title: Embargoed race-sequence CV plus fold-safe encoders
summary: Next-lap labels create overlap between adjacent laps of the same driver/race, so naive row-wise or lightly grouped CV can overstate AUC.
rationale: Next-lap labels create overlap between adjacent laps of the same driver/race, so naive row-wise or lightly grouped CV can overstate AUC. Time-series leakage literature and sklearn target-encoder docs both point to near-neighbor leakage and non-cross-fit encoders as common failure modes, especially with high-cardinality IDs like Driver and Race-Year.
implementation_hint: Split by ordered `(Race, Year, Driver)` sequences, purge at least 1 lap on each side of validation segments, and apply any target/frequency encoding with fold-local fit_transform only. Compare current CV to embargoed CV before trusting feature gains.
expected_effect: Likely lowers optimistic local CV a bit but improves leaderboard correlation and helps reject misleading features faster.
risk: May reduce apparent CV and make tuning noisier if folds become too small or too imbalanced.
